payment_modes.name: PREPAID
payment_modes.description: Prepaid plans require you to pay in advance for the services you use, offering flexibility without any contracts or fixed monthly fees. You can recharge as needed, making it easier to control spending and avoid unexpected bills. These plans are ideal for light or moderate users who want full control over their usage and costs.

payment_modes.name: MANUAL
payment_modes.description: Postpaid plans let you pay for services after using them, usually at the end of the billing cycle. They often come with a fixed monthly fee, may require a contract, and typically offer more data or call perks. These plans are ideal for users who prefer consistent billing and use a lot of data or make frequent calls, making them more cost-effective in such cases.

templates.name: Ubuntu 22F
templates.startup_script:
#cloud-config
package_update: true
package_upgrade: true
runcmd:
  - apt-get update -y
  - apt-get upgrade -y
  - reboot

templates.name: ActiveMQ
templates.startup_script:
#cloud-config
package_update: true
packages:
  - ansible
runcmd:
  - mkdir -p /usr/local/src/activemq-22-04/etc/systemd/system /usr/local/src/activemq-22-04/opt/cloudstack
  - cd /usr/local/src/activemq-22-04/etc/systemd/system && wget -q https://code.rahulbaweja.com/marketplace/_common-files/etc/systemd/system/activemq.service
  - cd /usr/local/src/activemq-22-04/opt/cloudstack && wget -q https://code.rahulbaweja.com/marketplace/_common-files/opt/cloudstack/activemq-cleanup.sh
  - cd /usr/local/src/activemq-22-04 && wget -q https://code.rahulbaweja.com/marketplace/activemq-22-04/activemq.yaml
  - ansible-playbook /usr/local/src/activemq-22-04/activemq.yaml

market_place_app.name: ActiveMQ
market_place_app.short_description: Apache ActiveMQ® is the most popular open source, multi-protocol, Java-based message broker. It supports industry standard protocols so users get the benefits of client choices across a broad range of languages and platforms.

templates.name: Docker
templates.startup_script:
#cloud-config
package_update: true
packages:
  - apt-transport-https
  - ca-certificates
  - curl
  - gnupg
  - lsb-release

runcmd:
  - curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg | gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg
  - echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/docker-archive-keyring.gpg] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(lsb_release -cs) stable" | tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list > /dev/null
  - apt update -y
  - apt install -y docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-compose-plugin
  - systemctl enable docker
  - systemctl start docker
  - usermod -aG docker ubuntu

market_place_app.name: Docker
market_place_app.short_description: Docker helps developers build, share, run, and verify applications anywhere — without tedious environment configuration or management.

market_place_app.name: Docker
market_place_app.short_description: Docker helps developers build, share, run, and verify applications anywhere — without tedious environment configuration or management.

templates.name: Guacamole
templates.startup_script:
#!/bin/bash

apt update -y
apt install -y software-properties-common net-tools curl wget

add-apt-repository --yes --update ppa:ansible/ansible
apt update -y
apt install -y ansible

#!/bin/bash

mkdir -p /opt/guacamole

cd /opt/guacamole &&  wget https://code.rahulbaweja.com/marketplace/guacamole/guacamole.yaml 

ansible-playbook guacamole.yaml

market_place_app.name: Guacamole
market_place_app.short_description: Apache Guacamole is a clientless remote desktop gateway. It supports standard protocols like VNC, RDP, and SSH.

templates.startup_script:

apt update -y
apt install -y software-properties-common net-tools curl wget

add-apt-repository --yes --update ppa:ansible/ansible
apt update -y
apt install -y ansible

#!/bin/bash

mkdir -p {/usr/local/src/mongodb-20-04/opt/cloudstack/,/usr/local/src/mongodb-20-04/}

cd /usr/local/src/mongodb-20-04/opt/cloudstack/ && wget https://code.rahulbaweja.com/marketplace/_common-files/opt/cloudstack/cleanup.sh

cd /usr/local/src/mongodb-20-04/ && wget https://code.rahulbaweja.com/marketplace/mongodb-20-04/mongodb8.yaml



ansible-playbook mongodb8.yaml
